title: Scaphyglottis kienastii
taxonomy: Scaphyglottis kienastii is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This species is further classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Cranichideae. The genus Scaphyglottis is a part of the large orchid family, with many species found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Scaphyglottis kienastii is native to Costa Rica.

intro: Scaphyglottis kienastii, also known as the Kienast's Scaphyglottis, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its small, white flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, Scaphyglottis kienastii has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Scaphyglottis kienastii is commonly used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other plants to enhance its therapeutic properties.
